subject: skills/psm-cross-version
verdict: The prior high is closed — the verify loop now has honest terminal states — leaving a clean, cohesive stateless migrator; the only residue is light repetition the fix pass introduced in the headless section.
summary: psm-cross-version is now structurally complete: architecture, agent-cohesion, and customization lenses all came back clean, and the lifecycle terminates on every branch (passed / partial / blocked / already-safe) with the load-bearing persona preserved as investment. Its remaining opportunities are minor — a mid-severity edge case (an empty risk map from a mis-pointed path reads the same as a clean module) and low-cost polish (the fix pass restated the never-false-pass gate and the partial/blocked triggers across sections).

agent_profile.name: psm-cross-version
agent_profile.title: PrestaShop cross-version migration engineer
agent_profile.mission: Turn one existing PrestaShop module into a single codebase that runs on 1.7.x, 8.x, and 9.x at once, via plan→confirm→apply→verify gated by psm-validate.

capabilities.name: Analisis: peta risiko per versi
capabilities.note: Reuses psm-validate's ps-static-scan.py as the deterministic per-version risk-map engine; empty map short-circuits to already-safe.

capabilities.name: Rencana perubahan
capabilities.note: Designs version-safe fixes from references/version-safe-patterns.md into .psm-cross-plan.md, a revisable, resumable artifact.

capabilities.name: Konfirmasi (gerbang)
capabilities.note: Blocks on Budi's approval before touching any file — un-skippable gate for irreversible source edits.

capabilities.name: Terapkan
capabilities.note: Applies approved changes with version_compare branches; git-checkpoint nudge first, stop-and-report partial state on apply failure.

capabilities.name: Verifikasi (gerbang wajib)
capabilities.note: psm-validate across all three versions; terminal blocked-state hands irreconcilable findings back to Budi instead of looping.

capabilities.name: Mode headless
capabilities.note: Non-interactive path returning honest passed/partial/blocked status with assumptions logged to memlog.

detailed_analysis.leanness: Lean and well-written; new guards are distinct, not mutually redundant. Two low-severity repetitions introduced by the fix pass: the never-false-pass gate and the partial/blocked triggers are each restated in the headless section.
detailed_analysis.architecture: Stateless single-capability skill wired correctly: clean topology, self-contained reference, sound linear activation spine, no structural defects.
detailed_analysis.determinism: Determinism-clean by design: risk-map and final gate both delegated to scripts, prompt reserved for fix-design judgment; only leak is the known, suite-wide-deferred config-parse.
detailed_analysis.customization: Stateless skill within the psm module: config.yaml (section psm, read at activation) is the sole and correct mechanism; no customize.toml is the deliberate, consistent sibling pattern, not a defect.
detailed_analysis.enhancement: Prior-pass additions close their gaps cleanly; two remaining experience gaps for the wrong-path invoker and the release-handoff moment.
detailed_analysis.agent-cohesion: Persona and capabilities cohere tightly; lifecycle now verified complete end-to-end with every branch terminating — no dead-ends.

journeys.name: Interactive migration
journeys.steps: Activate → resolve module+versions → risk-map scan (empty → already-safe exit) → draft plan → Budi approves → git nudge → apply (fail → partial) → psm-validate ×3 (irreconcilable → blocked) → summarize

journeys.name: Resume
journeys.steps: Activate → detect .psm-cross-plan.md → continue from last per-change status instead of re-analyzing

journeys.name: Headless (workflow/agent caller)
journeys.steps: Args in → scan → plan → apply without confirm gate → validate → one-line summary + plan/memlog paths + honest passed/partial/blocked status

experience.headless: Well-formed with honest terminal statuses (passed/partial/blocked); assumptions logged to memlog. Minor: restates the partial/blocked trigger conditions already defined at their guard sites.

themes.title: Fix-pass repetition in the headless section
themes.root_cause: The prior fix added terminal states (partial/blocked) at their guard sites (Terapkan, Verifikasi) and then re-derived both the trigger conditions and the never-false-pass gate rule again in the headless section, so the same facts now live in multiple places. Low cost, no behavior change, but the headless section should reference the guard sites as the single source rather than re-state them.
themes.action: In the headless section, name the three return tokens (passed/partial/blocked) as the return contract but drop the re-stated trigger conditions and the re-quoted gate rule — let Terapkan and Verifikasi remain the single source for when each state fires. Keep line 45 as the canonical gate and the Overview mention as legitimate role framing.

themes.title: Empty risk map is ambiguous (clean vs. wrong path)
themes.root_cause: The new empty-risk-map early exit reads a zero-finding scan as 'already cross-version-safe, done.' But a scan pointed at a non-module folder (wrong path, parent dir, stray php) also returns zero findings — so a mis-pointed user can get a false 'passes all three versions' with no plan. The confirmation psm-validate run partially guards this but the branch assumes it passes.
themes.action: Before taking the already-safe branch, confirm the scan actually saw a real module using the structure / ps_versions_compliancy fields the scan output already carries; if no module structure is detected, report 'no PrestaShop module found at <path>' and ask Budi to confirm the path. And require that if the confirmation psm-validate run is not green, the agent must not report success.

strengths: Prior high resolved: the verify loop now has honest terminal states (blocked hands irreconcilable findings back to Budi; partial reports mid-apply failure; empty-map exits to already-safe) — confirmed closed by both the cohesion and enhancement lenses, with no dead-ends remaining.
Load-bearing persona preserved: the careful-migration-engineer voice, 'operator holds decisions' framing, and design rationale are investment, not waste — untouched by the fix pass.
Zero rule duplication: reuses psm-validate's ps-static-scan.py as the risk-map engine and psm-validate as the final gate, so version rules live only in ps-rules.json.

Clean architecture and customization: correct stateless topology, one well-named references/ carve-out, self-contained reference file, and the deliberate no-customize.toml pattern consistent with sibling psm skills.
Safety architecture intact: un-skippable approval gate before irreversible edits, git-checkpoint nudge, mandatory tri-version validation before declaring done.

recommendations.action: Guard the empty-risk-map exit against a mis-pointed path: verify real module structure before declaring already-safe, and never report success if the confirmation psm-validate run is not green.

De-duplicate the headless section: name passed/partial/blocked as the return contract but reference the guard sites for their trigger conditions instead of re-stating them; drop the re-quoted gate rule.

At an all-green finish, offer to remove .psm-cross-plan.md (or flag it for release exclusion) so the shipped module is clean; keep it an offer since it is also the resume source. Optionally fold the config-parse into the shared psm config-loader cleanup.

findings.title: Distinguish 'no module found' from 'clean module' before declaring already-safe
findings.location: SKILL.md, 'Analisis: peta risiko per versi' — the empty-risk early exit
findings.evidence: The empty-risk-map exit reads a zero-finding scan as 'module sudah cross-version-safe ... selesai.' But a zero-finding map can also mean the scan ran over a folder that is not a PrestaShop module (wrong path, parent dir, stray php files) — the same empty result. The confirmation psm-validate run partially guards this, but the branch assumes that run passes and gives no instruction for the case where it errors on a non-module, so a mis-pointed user can receive a false 'already passes all three versions, done' with no risk map and no plan.
findings.recommendation: Before taking the empty-risk-map success branch, confirm the scan actually saw a real module using the structure / ps_versions_compliancy fields the scan output already carries (main module file present, valid structure). If no module structure is detected, report 'no PrestaShop module found at <path>' and ask Budi to confirm the path instead of declaring cross-version-safe. Also state that if the confirmation psm-validate run does not come back green, the agent must not report success.

findings.title: Never-false-pass gate rule stated four times
findings.evidence: The rule 'jangan menyatakan lolos sebelum psm-validate hijau di ketiga versi' appears at line 10 (Overview: 'buktikan lolos di ketiga versi'), line 45 (the canonical gate: 'hanya bila lolos psm-validate di 1.7.x, 8.x, dan 9.x'), line 47 ('menjaga jangan pernah menyatakan lolos palsu'), and line 53 ('Tetap berlaku: jangan menyatakan lolos sebelum psm-validate hijau...'). Line 45 is the load-bearing statement; the others re-assert it without adding a constraint.
findings.recommendation: Keep the gate at line 45 and the Overview mention at 10 as legitimate role framing. Drop the re-quote at 47 and the restatement at 53; where 53 needs it, reference the gate rather than re-deriving the rule.

findings.title: Exit-state trigger conditions re-derived in headless section
findings.evidence: The partial-status condition ('bila sebuah perubahan gagal diterapkan') is defined at line 41 and re-derived verbatim at line 53; the blocked-status condition ('bila ada temuan yang tak punya jalur version-safe') is defined at line 47 and re-derived at line 53. The status tokens (passed/partial/blocked) as a headless return enumeration are legitimately headless-specific, but the when-each-fires logic already lives at its guard site.
findings.recommendation: In the headless section name the three return tokens as the return contract but drop the re-stated trigger conditions — let the Terapkan (41) and Verifikasi (47) sections remain the single source for when partial/blocked arise.

findings.title: Offer to clear the .psm-cross-plan.md working artifact at successful handoff
findings.evidence: The overview promises 'module siap rilis ke siapa pun tanpa perubahan lagi,' yet the working artifact is written to <module-path>/.psm-cross-plan.md — inside the module that ships. On an all-green finish the transform is done but a build-process dotfile lingers in the release folder, so the module is not quite ship-clean and Budi is left to notice and remove it.
findings.recommendation: In the final all-passed summary, offer a single next move: remove .psm-cross-plan.md now that all three versions pass, or note it should be excluded from the release build (gitignore / build exclude). Keep it an offer, not an automatic delete, since it is also the resume source. In headless, keep returning the path but flag it as a working artifact to exclude from the release.

findings.title: On-Activation config parse is a prompt-side extraction leak (known/deferred)
findings.evidence: 'Muat config dari {project-root}/_bmad/config.yaml (+ .user.yaml bila ada). Ambil versi target dari section psm (psm_target_versions, default 1.7.8,8.1,9.0). Komunikasi dalam communication_language...' — the model parses YAML and extracts keyed values on every invocation.
findings.recommendation: Extraction/parsing category: reading a known YAML shape and pulling fixed keys has one correct answer per input, so it belongs in Python (a shared psm config-loader emitting compact JSON) rather than a per-run prompt parse. Acknowledged as the deliberately-deferred psm-suite convention cleanup; kept low because the cost is small and it is tracked suite-wide — flagged only for lens completeness, not as a new defect.
